title: WATCH: Bagpiper performs on St. Patrick’s Day in Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Bagpiper Nate Silva performed at Harry's Bar and Grill in Boston on St. Patrick’s Day.
Nate Silva played at over 30 locations in four days, including multiple stops at Harry’s bars.
Silva, known as @passionpiping, has been a bagpiper since age 11 and performs at about 100 events annually.

title: 50 ways to make the most of spring in Massachusetts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: DiParma Italian Table in West Yarmouth closes after over 20 years; a new restaurant to open in April.
Boston hotels increase prices amid World Cup demand; Airbnb offers new host bonuses in Massachusetts and Rhode Island.
JetBlue cancels Boston to Asheville route, impacting customers receive refunds; route initially launched in 2022.

title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady appeared on 'The Tonight Show' to discuss his upcoming flag football debut at the Fanatics Flag Football Classic in LA.
Brady reflected on his post-retirement life, mentioning his card-collecting business and his FOX broadcasting role.
Originally slated for Riyadh, the flag football event was moved to Los Angeles due to Middle East conflicts.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Over 30 TSA employees in New England have quit amid a government shutdown; Logan Airport remains operational with no long security lines.
Massport is implementing a new camera system to display TSA wait times, currently testing in Terminal B at Logan Airport.
Nationwide TSA worker absence rate rose to 10% during the shutdown, causing concerns for future staffing at Logan Airport.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lianes Brandao, 28, died after a fight and hit-and-run in Brockton early Sunday morning, according to authorities.
Police found Brandao in a parking lot at Lincoln Street and West Railroad Ave; he was later pronounced dead in Boston.
Witnesses say Brandao was involved in a fight before being run over by a gray 2013 Jeep Compass, which fled the scene.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, 36, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Ellie Fund.
Haberek was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer while pregnant in 2024.
The Ellie Fund supported her family during treatment, prompting her marathon participation.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, a new Korean restaurant in Allston, is part of a trio with Bombbada and Jinjee opening soon.
Sanbada offers grilled fish sets with banchan, rice, and unlimited ice cream, emphasizing ocean-inspired dishes.
Bombbada will open in July for late-night dining until 2 a.m., while Jinjee BBQ is set for a mid-April launch.

title: The 5 most exciting books to read this spring — what’s on your list?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: "Sweetbitter Song" by Rosie Hewlett retells Melantho and Penelope's story from Homer's "Odyssey" with a historical, sapphic twist.
"Under Water" by Tara Menon explores friendship and loss over decades, against the backdrop of natural disasters.
"Yesteryear" by Caro Claire Burke is a psychological thriller about a modern influencer trapped in 1855's reality.

title: Vermont Green FC upset Portland Hearts of Pine in regional rivalry U.S. Open Cup clash
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Vermont Green FC defeated Portland Hearts of Pine 1-0 in a U.S. Open Cup match in Burlington, Vermont.
David Ismail scored the only goal in the 40th minute, securing Vermont's victory in front of a sold-out Virtue Field.
Vermont Green FC advanced to the second round of the U.S. Open Cup, facing opponents yet to be determined.

title: Police arrest 15-year-old in East Boston stabbing after he allegedly returns to the crime scene for his cellphone
author: John Waller
bullets: Police arrested a 15-year-old boy in East Boston after he allegedly returned to a crime scene for his cellphone.
Two suspects wearing ski masks reportedly stabbed a male victim after he refused to surrender his belongings, including a scooter.
The victim was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, and a witness managed to disarm one suspect during the attack.
